scenarios.name: Ceasefire Holds + CPI Benign
scenarios.summary: Near-impossible now. Would require Trump to reverse blockade order AND Iran to stand down. Not the base case.

scenarios.name: Blockade Escalates — Crude Spikes
scenarios.summary: DOMINANT SCENARIO. Trump ordered formal Hormuz blockade. WTI $96 → $100-110. 2-day holiday gap into Wed F&O expiry. OMCs maximum pain. Watch 23,500 → 23,200 → 22,800.

scenarios.name: CPI Surprise + Rate Cut Killed
scenarios.summary: CPI prints hot tomorrow (Apr 13). Secondary to geopolitical shock but compounds the bear case. Banks, NBFCs, real estate all pressured.

signals.rationale: Works in ALL 3 scenarios. Defensive FMCG, zero crude sensitivity, 3%+ dividend yield floor. In risk-off Wednesday gap, acts as a natural anchor. Agri + hotels + cigarettes = diversified earnings with no import cost exposure.
signals.risk: Lower beta — lags cyclicals if S1 (ceasefire holds) materialises. Accept as cost of insurance.

signals.rationale: Upgraded 4→5. Talks collapse is exactly the scenario this pair was built for. Physical Brent $131.97 vs WTI $96.57 — $35 spot premium screams real supply stress. ONGC: every $1 crude rise = ~Rs 400-500 Cr EBITDA. BPCL: GRMs at -$9.4, losing money on every barrel. Long 2 ONGC : 1 BPCL short.
signals.risk: Surprise ceasefire reinstatement (low probability). Crude drop on OPEC+ supply surge. Wednesday holiday gap could force unwinds — size appropriately.

signals.rationale: Blockade order = maximum urgency. 23,500 PE for base case gap (22,800-23,200). Add 23,000 PE for Hormuz military incident tail (22,000-22,500). 72-hour holiday gap window before Wednesday open is now the most dangerous setup of this cycle. Cost: 0.5-1% of portfolio. Payout: 8-20x on blockade escalation.
signals.risk: Total premium loss if Trump reverses blockade (low probability). Decay accelerates after Tuesday night.

signals.rationale: Reduced 3→2. Blockade order means banking sector will be sold indiscriminately at Wednesday open regardless of HDFC-specific merit. Earnings thesis (NIM >3.65% Apr 18) still intact long-term but entry before Wednesday is poor timing. Wait for gap to settle post-expiry. Revisit Apr 16-17 at lower levels.
signals.risk: Wednesday gap-down could take HDFC to Rs 680-700 range before bouncing. Do not catch falling knife before Wednesday.

signals.rationale: Talks collapse + crude spike will pressure USD/INR from 93 toward 94-95. Every 1% INR depreciation adds ~0.8-1% to IT revenue in INR terms. Hold existing positions. Do not add ahead of Infosys Apr 23 results — guidance is the binary.
signals.risk: S2 risk-off hits IT via discretionary spend cuts. TCS sold off on 29% beat — guidance tone matters more than numbers. Do not overweight.

signals.rationale: NEW. Talks collapse + high crude = energy security premium on domestic alternatives. Zero import cost, govt pricing support, ~6% dividend yield. Direct substitute demand if gas/LNG spikes. One of few large-caps where S2 (ceasefire cracks) is a tailwind, not headwind. FII underweight — room for domestic re-rating.
signals.risk: Regulatory overhang on coal pricing and e-auctions. ESG exclusion limits re-rating ceiling. Lower conviction in S1 (ceasefire holds).

signals.rationale: Upgraded 4→5. Blockade order = ATF cost shock guaranteed. Jet fuel 35-40% of operating cost. WTI $96 → $100-110 wipes out quarterly profit. USD/INR pressure adds lease cost. Wednesday gap down is the entry — do not short before open, let gap establish then add. Full position size now warranted.
signals.risk: Partial fuel hedges may cushion one quarter. Crude reversal on surprise blockade reversal is tail risk. Hold through earnings season for full thesis to play out.

avoid.reason: Talks collapse removes primary recovery catalyst. GRMs at -$9.4 = structural losses on current runs. Physical Brent $35 premium over WTI confirms real supply stress. Catching a falling knife vs geopolitical macro headwind. Revisit only on: (a) credible new talks OR (b) GRMs recovering above $4-5.

6% weekly gain was built on ceasefire optimism — that thesis partially unwound Sunday evening. Market hasn't priced talks failure yet (closed Mon holiday). Asymmetry inverted: upside needs new catalyst, downside just needs absence of positive catalyst. Risk/reward 2:1 unfavourable from 24,050.

NEW. Crude derivatives (TiO2, pigments, solvents) = 55-60% of raw material costs. Sustained WTI $96+ compresses margins after a recovery quarter. Market priced in benign crude — talks collapse changes that. Avoid new longs until crude trajectory clarifies post-Wednesday.

NEW. Double headwind: (1) Hot CPI risk tomorrow (>3.5% kills June rate cut narrative, directly hits valuations). (2) Geopolitical risk-off pushes rate cut expectations further out. Most rate-sensitive sector, most vulnerable to FII outflows. Wrong timing to add.

avoid.stock: NBFCs — high wholesale funding (Bajaj Finance, Shriram, Muthoot leverage plays)
avoid.reason: NEW. Hot CPI removes rate cut catalyst. Wholesale funding costs stay elevated. These rallied on rate cut anticipation — sharpest correction if RBI signals pause-for-longer. Tactical avoid; reassess post-CPI print tomorrow.

calendar.event: HDFC Bank + ICICI Bank Q4 results

tracker.title: US-Iran Ceasefire & Strait of Hormuz
tracker.india_impact: CRITICAL: Trump ordered formal US Navy blockade of Hormuz (Apr 12, ~18:00 IST) — active military choke on 20% of global oil/LNG. Talks already collapsed earlier. Ceasefire probability now 5%. WTI $96.57, expect spike to $100-110 at Asian open. OMCs maximum pain Wednesday. Nifty gap risk extreme: 23,500 → 23,200 → 22,800.
tracker.watch_for: Iran response to blockade. WTI crossing $100. Wednesday Nifty open. Any Hormuz incident = gap to 22,800.

tracker.title: Q4 FY26 Earnings Season
tracker.india_impact: TCS beat but stock fell 2% — sell-the-news pattern confirmed. HDFC Bank Apr 18 is critical. Market needs earnings to justify 6% rally.
tracker.watch_for: HDFC Bank NIM >3.65% on Apr 18. Infosys FY27 guidance Apr 23.

tracker.title: Crude Oil Volatility
tracker.india_impact: Brent futures $95-97. Physical dated Brent $131.97 — extreme spot/futures divergence showing real supply stress. GRMs collapsed to -$9.4. Talks failure adds upside crude risk.
tracker.watch_for: Crude futures breaking $100. Hormuz shipping lanes. OPEC+ supply response.

tracker.title: RBI Monetary Policy
tracker.india_impact: Held at 5.25%. March CPI release Apr 13 (tomorrow) is key for June cut hopes. Consensus 3.4%.
tracker.watch_for: March CPI print Apr 13. Hot print (>3.6%) kills rate cut path.

tracker.title: FII Flows & Rupee
tracker.india_impact: 6% weekly rally suggests FII return. USD/INR stable at 93. Talks failure may pressure rupee at Wednesday open.
tracker.watch_for: Daily FII/DII data. USD/INR at Wednesday open.
